fullText: Letter from the saint Papa to the queen Helena Edited by: Annunziata Di Rienzo Preface This text is a born-digital edition of the Letter from the saint Papa to the queen Helena . The letter is part of a corpus of eight letters representing the correspondence of Papa bar Aggai, early 4th-century bishop of Seleucia, with some exponents of the western political and religious power. Beside the Letter from the saint Papa to the queen Helena , the corpus includes: a letter from Eusebius of Rome to Papa, a letter from Judah Kyriakos bishop of Jerusalem to Papa, a letter of the Empress Helena to Papa, a letter from Jacob bishop of Nisibis to Papa, a letter from Ephrem to Papa, a letter from Papa to the citizens of Nisibis, the letter sent by the Western Fathers on the illegal removal of Papa. This edition is based on the following manuscript witnesses: Erbil 509 (olim Alqoš 169/ Baghdad 509), f. 71v-72r (E) (the most ancient and principal source, probably direct or indirect source for the later extant witnesses); Rome, Vatican Library, Borg. Sir. 82, f. 115v-116r; 109v (B); Birmingham, Mingana Collection Syr. 47, f. 35v-36r (M 1); Birmingham, Mingana Collection Syr. 586, f. 104r/v (M 3); Trichur, Chaldean Syrian Church, APSTCH THRI 65, f. 48v-49r (A) (the manuscript has been digitized, and it can be viewed here ); Baghdad, Chaldean Catholic Church. Patriarchate of Baghdad 377, f. 13v/14v (C) (the manuscript has been digitized, and it can be viewed here ). This text has never been published in any prior edition. This edition should not be considered as definitive but as a still in-progress material. There is an old German translation of the entire corpus (based on the Syriac text preserved in ms Borg. Sir. 82) in O. Braun, "Der Briefwechsel des Katholikos Papa von Seleucia. Ein Beitrag zur Geschichte der ostsyrischen Kirche im vierten Jahrhundert", Zeitschrift für katholische Theologie 18 (1894), pp. 163-182, 546-565. An English translation is provided along with the Syriac text. This edition is an outcome of the Marie Skłodowska-Curie Global Fellowship 2022 – Project "ForM - FORGED MEMORIES: the Church of Late Antique Iran and the West", funded by the European Commission. Text ܕܡܼܢ ܩܕܝܫܐ ܦܦܐ ܠܘܬ ܗܹܠܹܢܐ ܡܠܟܬܐ. ܠܐܡܐ ܒܪܝܟܬܐ ܡܠܟܬܐ ܡܗܝܡܢܬܐ ܗܹܠܹܢܐ: ܦܦܐ ܥܒܕܗ ܕܝܫܘܥ ܘܣܵܓܘܕܐ ܘܡܝܩܪܢܐ ܕܨܠܝܒܗ܆ ܫܠܵܡ. ܐܝܠܝܢ ܡܪܬܝ ܡܠܟܬܐ ܕܬܒܥܬܝ ܡܸܢܢ܆ ܠܐ ܣܦܩܝܢ ܙܘܥܝܢ̈ ܕܢܕܪܟ ܐܲܝܕܐ ܕܥܵܕܪܐ܇ ܘܢܟܬܘܒ ܠܟܝ. ܡܛܠ ܕܟܪ̈ܣܛܝܢܐ ܕܦܢܝܬܢ ܒܡܣܟܢܘܬܐ ܡܫܬܒܗܪܝܢ. ܘܒܐܣܘܪ̈ܐ ܘܒܐܘܠܨ̈ܢܐ ܕܓܢܸܣ ܓܢܸܣ. ܘܟܘܪܚܐ ܙܥܘܪܐ܆ ܪܘܝܼܚ ܗ̄ܘܼ ܠܒܝܬ ܨܠܘܬܐ ܕܝܠܢ. ܘܠܘ ܡܛܠ ܕܐܝܬܝܢ ܚܒܢ̈ܢܐ ܘܡܩܛ̈ܥܐ ܡܼܢ ܒܢܝ̈ܢܐ܇ ܐܠܐ ܡܛܠ ܕܠܐ ܢܥܘܠ ܥܠܲܝܢ ܚܣܡܐ ܕܚܢ̈ܦܐ ܒܝܕ ܒܢܝ̈ܢܐ ܪܘܪ̈ܒܐ܇ ܘܡܬܒܨܪ ܬܘܠܡܕܐ ܕܟܪܣܛܝܢܘܬܐ ܒܩܘܢܕܝܢܘܣ ܕܪ̈ܕܘܦܝܐ ܕܡܩܝܡܝܢ ܥܠܝܢ. ܘܐܦ ܬܫܡܫܬܐ ܕܡܢܟܘܢ ܐܫܬܕܪܬܸ ܠܢ܆ ܐܠܝܼܨܝܼܢܢ ܒܢܛܘܪܬܗܿ. ܘܕܢܦܩܝܗܿ ܠܓܠܝܐ ܘܢܫܡܫ ܒܗܿ ܆ ܕܚܠܝܢܢ ܕܠܡܐ ܒܥܠܠܬ̈ܐ ܡܬܢܣܒܐ ܡܢܢ. ܘܠܐ ܗ̄ܘܼܵܐ E corrupted/lacuna; M 3 ܗܵܕܹܐ ܒܠܚܘܕ ܡܼܢ ܚܢ̈ܦܐ ܩܢܛܝܢܢ܆ ܐܠܐ ܐܦ ܡܼܢ ܝܗܘ̈ܕܝܼܐ ܘܡܪ̈ܩܝܘܢܐ. ܕܡܼܢ ܕܐܬܩܦܚܘ ܒܐܘܚܕܢܟܘܢ ܒܫܘܠܛܢܐ ܕܡܠܟܘܬܟܘܢ܆ ܠܦܢܝܬܢ BC ܠܦܢܝܼܬܵܐ ; E corrupted/lacuna; M 3 ܠܣܲܢܝܘܼ ܗܕܐ ܥܪܩܘ ܘܐܬܼܘ ܡܼܢ ܩܕܡ ܦܘܩܕܢܟܼܘܢ܆ ܣܢܐܬܐ ܡܫܡܠܝܬܐ ܐܘܣܸܦܘ E corrupted/lacuna; B om. ܘܣܢܼܘ ܠܢ . ܘܒܟܠ ܙܒܢ ܡܪܟܒܝܢ ܥܠܝܢ ܥ̈ܠܠܬܐ ܕܓܠܬ̈ܐ. ܘܗܘܝܢ ܠܢ ܩܛܐܓܪ̈ܢܐ ܒܝܬ ܕܝ̈ܢܐ ܥܠܡܝ̈ܢܝܐ. ܚܢܢ ܗܵܠܝܹܢ ܕܠܘܬܢ ܕܢܟܬܘܒ ܘܢܘܕܥ ܠܟܝ ܐܝܟ ܟܪܝܼܗܐ ܕܡܚܘܐ ܟܐܒܗ ܘܟܘܪܗܢܗ ܠܐܣܝܐ. ܘܡܐ ܕܚܼܙܐ ܐܣܝܐ ܚܟܝܡܐ ܐܢ ܐܝܬ ܥܠܘܗܝ ܣܒܪܐ E corrupted/lacuna; AM 3 om (blank space); M 1 ܣܲܡܵܐ ܗܸܘ ܝܿܕܿܥ ܕܢܥܒܕ ܠܗ ܐܣܝܘܬܐ. ܗܵܠܝܹܢ ܕܬܚܸܝܬܼ AM 3 om (blank space); C ܕܲܬܼܚܘܿܬܼ ܫܘܠܛܢܢ ܣܝ̈ܡܢ ܒܦܢܝ̈ܬܢ ܒܦܣܝ̈ܩܬܐ ܪܫܡܢܢ ܐܢܝ̈ܢ ܠܡܠܟܘܬܟܝ. ܘܫܘܠܛܢܐ ܘܚܟܡܬܐ ܘܕܪܥܐ ܘܓܢܒܪܘܬܐ ܘܙܟܘܬܐ ܕܡܫܝܚܐ ܆ ܠܘܬܟܘܢ E corrupted/lacuna; AM 3 om (blank space); M 1 ܐܸܢܘܿܢ ; . ܐܢ̄ܬܘܢ ܙܕܩܿ ܕܬܒܚܪܘܢ ܒܝܕܥܬܟܘܢ ܘܬܕܥܘܢ E corrupted/lacuna; AM 3 om (blank space); M 1 ܘܲܬܿܬܲܪܨܘܼܢ ; C presents two alternative versions: ܘܲܬܕܘܼܢܘܼܢ (put into brackets) or ܘܲܬܕܲܒܿܪܘܼܢ ܒܫܘܠܛܢܐ. ܘܐܿܝܕܐ ܕܝܕܥܝܢ ܐܢ̄ܬܘܢ ܕܥܕܪܐ ܐܘܕܥܘܢ ܠܐܟܣܢܝܘܬܢ. ܡܿܪܬܝ ܠܨܒܝܢܐ ܛܒܐ E corrupted/lacuna; C ܛܲܢܵܢܵܐ ܕܡܠܟܘܬܟܘܢ ܩܒܠܢܢ. ܐܝܟ ܡܘܗܒܬܐ ܕܪܒܐ E corrupted/lacuna; AM 3 om (blank space) ܡܼܢ ܒܢܝܢܗܿ ܕܥܕܬܐ. ܘܐܦ ܒܟܘܢ ܫܒܚܝܢ E corrupted/lacuna; AM 3 om (blank space); M 1 C: ܫܲܒܲܚܢ ܠܐܠܗܐ ܡܪܝܐ ܡܫܝܚܐ: ܘܒܬܟܫܦܬܐ ܘܒܬܚܢܢܬܐ E corrupted; A ܘܲܒܼܬܼܵܘܕܝܼܬܼܵܐ ܘܒܚܘܒܐ ܘܒܕܚܠܬܐ ܡܼܢ ܐܲܢܘܼܣܝܵܐ ܕܠܒܐ M 1 ܕܠܒܢ ; E corrupted: ...ܕܠܒ ܒܥܕܬܗ ܕܐܠܗܐ ܨܠܘܬܐ ܥܠܲܝܟܘܢ ܩܪܒܝܢܢ EA: ...ܩܪܒ ; BM 1 C ܩܲܪܸܒܼܢܲܢ [.] M 1 C add (E presents a lacuna here, A a blank space) ܘܝܬܝܪܐܝܬ܆ ܐܢܐ ܦܦܐ ܡܚܝܼܠܐ ܘܚܛܝܐ ܘܙܥܘܪܐ ܕܟܠܗܘܢ ܬܠܡܝ̈ܕܘܗܝ ܕܡܫܝܚܐ ܒܕܡ̈ܥܐ ܚܢܝ̈ܓܬܐ ܡܬܟܫܦ ܐ̄ܢܐ ܠܗ ܠܡܪܝܐ E corrupted/lacuna; B ܠܡܪܝ ; M 3 ܠܡܫܝܚܐ ܕܗܘܸ ܢܦܩܘܕ Sic! Based on the meaning, this could be a wrong reading for ܢܦܪܘܣ (a second hand in C proposes this reading adding it in the line-spacing) or ܢܦܫܘܛ . The mistake should have happened in E (or before). ܝܡܝܢܗ ܥܠܝܟܘܢ. ܘܢܦܩܘܕ ܠܡܠܐܟܘ̈ܗܝ ܩܕ̈ܝܫܐ ܆ ܕܢܢܛܪܘܢܟܘܢ ܒܟܠܗܝܢ E corrupted/lacuna; M 3 ܒܟܼܠ ܐܘܪ̈ܚܬܟܘܢ. ܘܐܝܟ ܪܥܝܐ ܕܡܫܩܠ ܠܐܡܪ̈ܘܗܝ E corrupted/lacuna; M 3 ܠܐ ܢܵܫܐ ܘܠܥܪ̈ܒܘܗܝ ܥܠ ܟܬܦܬ̈ܗ ܆ ܗܟܢܐ ܬܫܩܠܘܢ E corrupted/lacuna; A partially omits (ܬܫ then blank space); M 3 ܬܸܫܩܘܿܠ ܐܢܘܢ ܠܒܢܝ̈ܗܿ ܕܥܕܬܐ ܥܠ ܟܬ̈ܦܬܐ [ܕܫܘܠܛܟܘܢ B: ܕܫܘܠܛܵܢܐ ; Then B continues with a different text (from the Letters of Papa to the Nisibenes). The finale of the Letter of Papa to Helena can be found, in B, at f. 109v. At f. 109v a marginal note, in Latin, informs us: Desunt hic nonnulla quae vide pag. 228 Finis epistulae Papae ad imperatricem . The order of the letters in B is different (probably owe to the fact the leaves of the original manuscript were dismembered at that time). . ܘܟܠܗܘܢ ܡܗܝ̈ܡܢܐ ܒܬܘܩܢ ܡܠܟܘܬܟܘܢ ܢܬܚܝܠܘܢ. ܘܒܟܘܢ ܢܬܥܫܢܘܢ ܟܠܗܘܢ] This paragraphs is particularly damaged in E (AM 3 both leave blank spaces where E is illegible; B leaves a long blank space). ܟܪ̈ܣܛܝܵܢܹܐ ܥܡܪ̈ܝ ܬܒܝܠ܇ ܫܠܡܐ ܒܟܬܒܬܸ ܐܝܕܐ ܕܝܠܝ ܦܦܐ ܫܕܪܬ ܠܡܠܟܘܬܟܘܢ ܡܗܝܡܢܬܐ. ܨܠܝܒܗ ܕܦܪܘܩܢ ܡܫܝܚܐ : ܕܒܗ ܐܬܬܪܝܡܬܸ ܘܐܬܪܡܪܡܬܸ ܡܠܟܘܬܟܘܢ ܆ ܗܘܸ ܢܢܛܪ ܠܟܠܗܿ ܥܕܬܐ ܕܬܚܝܬܸ ܫܡܝܐ ܐܡܝܢ. Translation From the saint Papa to the queen Helena To the blessed mother, the believing queen Helena, Papa servant of Jesus and worshipper and devotee of His cross. Peace. My lady queen, these [things] that you seek from us, our mental faculties are not sufficient to reach [an idea of] what is useful and [then] to write to you, because the Christians of our region glorify themselves in poverty, chains and in [their] various sufferings. And a small hut is large as our house of prayer; and [this] not because we are lazy and negligent concerning the buildings, but so that the jealousy of the pagans does not act wickedly against us because of the greatness [of our] buildings; the catechizing of Christianity is absent because of the danger of persecutions raised against us. And we are compelled to keep safe the liturgical office that you sent to us, but we are afraid to offer it openly and to perform the office according to its rite, lest it is taken away from us with pretexts. And it is not only the pagans that we fear, but also the Jews and Marcionites, who, as they were oppressed in your jurisdiction from the authority of your kingdom, flee in this region of ours, and they came here before your decree. They have entirely increased enmity, and they hated us. And every time, they set against us clear pretexts, and they accuse us in the secular tribunals. We briefly delineated to your majesty these things about us – in order to write and let you know, as one who is sick with a disease [informs] his physician of his pain and his illness; and when the wise physician see if there is hope M 1 : medicine for him, he knows the cure that is good for him – those [things] that are put under your authority in our regions. And the authority, the wisdom, the arm, the heroism and the victory of Christ [are] with you. It is right for you to examine according to your knowledge and to understand C: to (judge)/manage; M 1 : to be directed according to your authority. Inform us who are foreigners what you think you could do to help. My lady, we receive the good will of your majesty as a gift greater than building churches. And also in you we glorify God Lord Christ, and with supplications, fervent prayer A: thanksgiving , love and with the fear that comes from the anxiety of the heart, we offer a prayer for you in the Church of God; and especially me, the poor and sinner Papa, and the least of all the disciples of Christ, with sad tears I supplicate the Lord to command ( sic Probably mistaken reading for: "to stretch out" ) his right hand upon you, and to command to his holy angels to guard you in all your ways (cf. Ps 91 [90], 11), and as a shepherd who carries his lambs and his sheep on his shoulders, so you will carry the sons of the Church on the shoulders of your authority. And all the believers, in the institution of your regality, will take courage and in you will be strengthened all the Christians who inhabits the habitable world. A salutation, through a writing of my own hand, I, Papa, send to your believing majesty. May the Cross of our Savior Christ, through which your majesty has been exalted and became great, guard the whole church that is under the heaven. Amen.
title:
Letter from the saint Papa to the queen
                    Helena
The Letters of Papa bar Aggai
Digital Syriac Corpus
